evals.prompt: I want to deploy uptime-kuma to Sealos, GitHub: https://github.com/louislam/uptime-kuma
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (7+), identifies Node.js project with external services, recommends proceeding with deployment

assertions.name: identifies-nodejs
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Node.js

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 and the result records the corresponding risk level

assertions.name: detects-external-db
assertions.description: Detects external database dependency (SQLite/MariaDB)

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report with dimensions and scores

assertions.name: creates-log-file
assertions.description: Creates a deploy log file at ~/.sealos/logs/

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy https://github.com/sharkdp/bat
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 entry judgment produces no separate result and silently continues to readiness scoring because a CLI project may still run as a Job, CronJob, worker, or service

assertions.name: identifies-cli-tool
assertions.description: Correctly identifies bat as a CLI tool / non-web application

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Does not emit a separate status, score, report, candidate list, or artifact for the entry judgment

assertions.name: continues-readiness-analysis
assertions.description: Silently continues into Phase 1

assertions.name: resolves-online-contract-later
assertions.description: Defers Job, CronJob, worker, or service feasibility to target selection and concrete build/runtime checks

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 entry judgment stays silent and continues to readiness scoring because the skills/tooling repository is not certainly impossible to deploy

assertions.name: identifies-skill-pack
assertions.description: Identifies the current repository as a skills pack or tooling repo rather than a standalone web app

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Creates no separate status, review report, score, candidate list, or artifact for the entry judgment

assertions.name: phase-one-runs
assertions.description: Immediately continues with normal Phase 1 analysis

evals.prompt: Help me deploy alist to Sealos, project URL: https://github.com/alist-org/alist
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (8+), identifies Go web application with REST API and WebDAV server, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-go
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Go language

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 and the result records the corresponding risk level

assertions.name: identifies-http-server
assertions.description: Identifies this as an HTTP server / web service

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.prompt: I want to deploy lobe-chat to Sealos: https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (10+), identifies Next.js monorepo with pnpm workspaces, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-nextjs
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Next.js

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 and the result records the corresponding risk level

assertions.name: detects-monorepo-or-workspace
assertions.description: Detects pnpm monorepo / workspace structure

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy https://github.com/jgraph/drawio-desktop
evals.expected_output: Because an Electron repository may have a Linux, web, container, or remote-desktop path, the Phase 1 entry judgment produces no separate result and silently continues to readiness scoring

assertions.name: identifies-desktop-app
assertions.description: Correctly identifies drawio-desktop as an Electron / desktop GUI application

assertions.name: does-not-stop-on-label
assertions.description: Does not stop merely because the repository is an Electron desktop application

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Emits no separate status or review report for the entry judgment

assertions.name: continues-phase-one
assertions.description: Silently starts normal readiness analysis

evals.prompt: Deploy vaultwarden to Sealos: https://github.com/dani-garcia/vaultwarden
evals.expected_output: Maximum readiness score (12/12), identifies Rust web application with Rocket framework and existing Dockerfile, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-rust
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Rust language

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 and the result records the corresponding risk level

assertions.name: identifies-web-service
assertions.description: Identifies this as a web service / HTTP server

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy https://github.com/pallets/flask
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 entry judgment produces no separate result and continues to readiness scoring because a framework/library repository is not certainly impossible to run online

assertions.name: identifies-library
assertions.description: Correctly identifies Flask as a framework/library, not a standalone deployable app

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Does not emit a separate library status, score, report, or artifact for the entry judgment

assertions.name: continues-analysis
assertions.description: Silently continues to Phase 1 and lets later concrete work establish the outcome

evals.prompt: Help me deploy mealie (home recipe management): https://github.com/mealie-recipes/mealie
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (8+), identifies Python FastAPI application with PostgreSQL support, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-python
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Python

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 and the result records the corresponding risk level

assertions.name: detects-external-db
assertions.description: Detects PostgreSQL database dependency

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-petclinic
evals.expected_output: Good readiness score (6+), identifies Java Spring Boot application with port 8080 and Actuator endpoints, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-java
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Java / Spring Boot

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 and the result records the corresponding risk level

assertions.name: identifies-web-service
assertions.description: Identifies this as a web application on port 8080

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy https://github.com/toeverything/affine — use the unchanged default-branch source. The refreshed official catalog contains one exact spec.gitRepo match.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 completes, Phase 1.5 records the unique trusted exact match, copies that official YAML verbatim, skips Phases 2–5.5, resolves required inputs in Phase 6, dry-runs and deploys with the same args, then completes Phase 6.5

assertions.name: runs-analysis-first
assertions.description: The entry judgment produces no separate result, and Phase 1 completes readiness analysis before catalog discovery

assertions.name: selects-unique-official-exact
assertions.description: Requires one exact spec.gitRepo match from a fresh official catalog with concrete commit provenance

assertions.name: materializes-verbatim-template
assertions.description: Copies the selected official YAML verbatim to .sealos/template/index.yaml and records decision.route as deploy_official_template

assertions.name: skips-build-and-generation
assertions.description: Skips Phases 2, 3, 4, 5, and 5.5 instead of detecting or building an image or generating another template

assertions.name: resolves-official-inputs
assertions.description: Collects missing required spec.inputs in Phase 6 without modifying the official YAML or exposing sensitive values

assertions.name: dry-runs-before-deploy
assertions.description: Uses the same official YAML and args for a successful dry-run before the real Template API request

assertions.name: runs-runtime-truth
assertions.description: Continues through Phase 6.5 and records the primary image found in the live deployment

evals.prompt: The application URL works, but Launchpad does not show a public address. Diagnose the Sealos template and live deployment.
evals.expected_output: Runs the Launchpad public-network check, validates numeric root Ingress backend port and Service port matching, compares the App URL host, and inventories manager-labeled resources before proposing a template repair.

assertions.name: runs-launchpad-network-check
assertions.description: Uses sealos-launchpad-network.mjs or the equivalent Launchpad API contract before declaring success

assertions.name: checks-numeric-ingress-port
assertions.description: Requires backend.service.port.number on the root Prefix Ingress and matches it to Service.spec.ports[].port

assertions.name: compares-app-url-host
assertions.description: Compares the App URL host with the Launchpad public or custom domain

assertions.name: inventories-live-resources
assertions.description: Checks manager-labeled Ingresses, Services, ready endpoints, App, Instance, and orphan resource footprint

assertions.name: protects-sensitive-data
assertions.description: Keeps kubeconfig, environment values, and Secret data out of diagnostic output

evals.prompt: Deploy this target: package.json has scripts.start='node server.js', dependencies.express='^5', and devDependencies.electron='^35'. There are no Electron scripts, desktop API imports, or desktop packaging files.
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 entry judgment does not stop or emit a separate result; readiness scoring identifies the Express start command and continues normal analysis

assertions.name: does-not-stop-on-dependency-alone
assertions.description: Does not stop solely because Electron appears in devDependencies

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Emits no separate status or report for the entry judgment

assertions.name: uses-server-entrypoint-in-phase-one
assertions.description: Phase 1 uses the Express dependency and node start command during normal analysis

evals.prompt: Generate the Sealos Template phase for a Mastodon-style app whose analysis classifies SMTP_PORT as an optional input with the default string "587". Expose it as spec.inputs.smtp_port, keep infrastructure replicas and ports numeric, and validate the generated artifact before deployment.
evals.expected_output: Generates a Template whose SMTP port default remains the YAML string "587", preserves numeric Kubernetes infrastructure fields, and runs the docker-to-sealos quality gate against .sealos/template/index.yaml so R052 blocks an unquoted default before deployment.

assertions.name: smtp-default-is-string
assertions.description: Emits spec.inputs.smtp_port.default as a quoted YAML string such as "587", never as the numeric scalar 587

assertions.name: input-reference-is-declared
assertions.description: Declares spec.inputs.smtp_port and references that declared input from the Mastodon SMTP_PORT environment value

assertions.name: infrastructure-numbers-stay-numeric
assertions.description: Keeps Kubernetes replicas, container ports, and Service ports as numeric YAML fields

assertions.name: validates-generated-artifact
assertions.description: Runs quality_gate.py with --artifacts pointing to .sealos/template/index.yaml before configuration or deployment

assertions.name: rejects-unquoted-input-default
assertions.description: Treats R052 or the equivalent string-type validation failure as blocking and fixes the template before continuing

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy 当前本地修改过的 https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat checkout，使用本地官方多组件 Compose；模板中 RAG 固定使用 OpenAI embeddings，并且 PostgreSQL 需要 vector 扩展。不要忽略本地改动，只允许使用该 skill 已有的产物。
evals.expected_output: Generates the existing Sealos template artifact with pinned component images, official health probes, valid runtime credentials, and a database final-state gate; the complete docker-to-sealos quality gate passes against the exact final template before deployment, with no new artifact type introduced

assertions.name: does-not-bypass-local-changes
assertions.description: Phase 1.5 records continue_standard_pipeline and does not replace the modified checkout with an official catalog template

assertions.name: pins-component-images
assertions.description: Accepts the exact declared selectors and resolves every emitted LibreChat component image to an immutable digest without pre-screening third-party architecture

assertions.name: uses-official-rag-health
assertions.description: Uses /health on port 8000 for LibreChat RAG probes instead of probing /

assertions.name: validates-format-constrained-credentials
assertions.description: Does not use bare random() defaults for LibreChat CREDS_KEY/CREDS_IV hex contracts

assertions.name: requires-selected-provider-credential
assertions.description: OpenAI embeddings is paired with a non-empty required OpenAI credential rather than an optional empty input

assertions.name: gates-rag-on-vector-state
assertions.description: RAG has an initContainer gate that waits for PostgreSQL and verifies the vector extension before startup

assertions.name: classifies-mongodb-as-database
assertions.description: Converts the official mongo:8.0.20 service into the pinned MongoDB KubeBlocks Cluster contract

assertions.name: rejects-raw-mongodb-workload
assertions.description: Emits no MongoDB Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, or standalone Service

assertions.name: does-not-assume-ghcr-private
assertions.description: Does not add imagePullSecrets solely because an image is hosted on GHCR

assertions.name: fails-closed-before-deploy
assertions.description: Runs the complete docker-to-sealos quality gate after final template mutation and does not deploy while the gate is failing

assertions.name: keeps-existing-artifact-inventory
assertions.description: Introduces no generated artifact type beyond the full deploy skill's existing artifact inventory

evals.prompt: Deploy a repository with apps/desktop as an Electron app and apps/api as an Express service. Do not choose the deployment target yet.
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 entry judgment emits nothing and continues because the mixed repository is not certainly impossible; readiness analysis discovers the units and target selection happens before build or deployment

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Does not emit a separate mixed-project status, candidate list, review report, or artifact for the entry judgment

assertions.name: continues-to-analysis
assertions.description: Phase 1 discovers the Express API and Electron units

assertions.name: defers-target-selection
assertions.description: Resolves the exact unit before build or deployment, not inside the Phase 1 entry judgment

evals.prompt: Deploy a selected repository root whose only deployment contract is Dockerfile: FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2025. The target Sealos runtime is linux/amd64 and there are no other workload candidates.
evals.expected_output: One Windows Dockerfile does not establish that the whole project is impossible to deploy, so the Phase 1 entry judgment says nothing and continues to readiness scoring

assertions.name: does-not-overclaim
assertions.description: Does not treat one Windows container stage as proof that the project can never run online

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Emits no separate runtime status, review report, or artifact for the entry judgment

assertions.name: continues-phase-one
assertions.description: Silently continues to normal project analysis

evals.prompt: The selected project is a confirmed Windows-only native WPF desktop application. It requires an interactive Windows desktop and Win32 APIs, and has no server, web, remote-desktop, container, source-porting, or alternative runtime target. Deploy it unchanged to a Sealos linux/amd64 cluster.
evals.expected_output: At the start of Phase 1, gives one short concrete explanation that this Windows-only desktop application cannot run on the Sealos Linux runtime, then stops before scoring or producing a status object, report, candidate list, or file

assertions.name: stops-certainly-impossible-project
assertions.description: Stops at Phase 1 entry because the selected project is unambiguously Windows-only and has no online runtime path

assertions.name: explains-concisely
assertions.description: Names the Windows desktop versus Sealos Linux incompatibility in one short explanation

assertions.name: no-phase-artifact-or-assessment
assertions.description: Creates no status object, score, review report, candidate list, or project file

evals.prompt: A deployment target is not obviously impossible to run on Sealos, but Phase 1 gives it a readiness score of 2/12. The user already asked to deploy it. Continue the sealos-deploy lifecycle.
evals.expected_output: The Phase 1 entry judgment emits nothing; readiness scoring reports the severe risks, then the pipeline proceeds to image detection and guarded containerization until a concrete build, safety, validation, or runtime check fails

assertions.name: no-entry-check-output
assertions.description: Produces no separate status, report, score, or artifact for the entry judgment

assertions.name: reports-low-score-risk
assertions.description: Explains the 2/12 findings and likely failure modes clearly

assertions.name: continues-after-score
assertions.description: Continues to Phase 2 and later guarded work because the user requested deployment

assertions.name: keeps-concrete-gates
assertions.description: Still stops on actual safety, image, build, template, or runtime failures

evals.prompt: Re-run /sealos-deploy for a project whose .sealos/state.json records a live last_deploy image and URL. The current root Dockerfile contains a Windows stage, but the existing application is running on Sealos.
evals.expected_output: Mode detection verifies the existing live deployment and enters the UPDATE lifecycle without running Phase 1

assertions.name: recognizes-project-can-run
assertions.description: Uses the existing live deployment as proof that the project can run

assertions.name: does-not-reject-update
assertions.description: Does not let a Windows stage signal block a previously verified live deployment

assertions.name: skips-phase-one
assertions.description: Skips Phase 1, including its entry judgment and readiness scoring, in UPDATE mode

assertions.name: enters-mode-detection
assertions.description: Continues to verify the live application and choose DEPLOY versus UPDATE

assertions.name: preserves-runtime-validation
assertions.description: Still verifies rollout and runtime truth during the update path

evals.prompt: Deploy an unchanged default-branch repository that has no exact spec.gitRepo entry in the official Sealos template catalog.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1.5 records continue_standard_pipeline and immediately continues to Phase 2 without looking for or using a similar template

assertions.name: records-no-exact-route
assertions.description: Writes decision.route as continue_standard_pipeline with a no-exact-match reason

assertions.name: continues-to-phase-two
assertions.description: Continues directly to existing-image detection

assertions.name: does-not-run-similarity
assertions.description: Does not structurally match, select, or reference similar templates

assertions.name: does-not-materialize-catalog-yaml
assertions.description: Does not copy any catalog YAML to .sealos/template/index.yaml

evals.prompt: The official catalog has an exact repository match, but the current local checkout is dirty and on a custom branch. Deploy the current source without losing those changes.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1.5 explains that the exact official template cannot replace the current checkout, records continue_standard_pipeline, and builds from the current source

assertions.name: preserves-current-source-intent
assertions.description: Does not silently deploy the official release while ignoring local or branch changes

assertions.name: disables-official-reuse
assertions.description: Invokes exact matching with official-template reuse disabled

assertions.name: explains-standard-route
assertions.description: Briefly explains why the exact match continues through the standard pipeline

assertions.name: does-not-copy-official-template
assertions.description: Leaves .sealos/template/index.yaml for Phase 5 generation

evals.prompt: Deploy a project while the official template catalog cannot be refreshed and no trustworthy non-stale cache is available.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1.5 records an unavailable catalog result and continues to Phase 2 without blocking deployment or attempting similarity matching

assertions.name: catalog-failure-is-nonblocking
assertions.description: Catalog network, Git, or parse failure does not stop the deployment

assertions.name: records-unavailable-decision
assertions.description: Writes continue_standard_pipeline with an unavailable-catalog reason when possible

assertions.name: continues-to-phase-two
assertions.description: Proceeds with current-source image detection and later phases

assertions.name: does-not-use-stale-template
assertions.description: Does not directly deploy an untrusted or stale catalog YAML

evals.prompt: Deploy a fresh, unchanged default-branch clone with one exact official template match. The repository does not ignore .sealos/, and Phase 1 has just created the untracked .sealos/analysis.json.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1.5 ignores only its own generated analysis artifact when checking source cleanliness, selects the unique freshly verified official template, and takes the Phase 6 fast path

assertions.name: does-not-misclassify-generated-analysis
assertions.description: Does not treat the workflow-created .sealos/analysis.json as a user source change

assertions.name: still-detects-real-overrides
assertions.description: Would still disable reuse for any other tracked or untracked change, including .sealos/config.json

assertions.name: takes-exact-fast-path
assertions.description: Records deploy_official_template and skips Phases 2 through 5.5

evals.prompt: Resume a first deployment whose local .sealos/template-references.json claims verified_for_reuse=true and whose copied YAML claims an official exact match, but the official catalog cannot be freshly fetched or verified now.
evals.expected_output: The resumed Phase 1.5 refuses to trust the local claim or YAML, records continue_standard_pipeline, removes only a byte-identical prior official materialization when safe, and continues to Phase 2

assertions.name: requires-fresh-remote-proof
assertions.description: Reruns official origin, commit, and checkout verification instead of trusting local verified_for_reuse metadata

assertions.name: does-not-deploy-forged-local-yaml
assertions.description: Does not enter Phase 6 with only the local artifact or copied template as evidence

assertions.name: continues-standard-route
assertions.description: Falls back non-blockingly to Phase 2 when fresh verification is unavailable

evals.prompt: The real Template API deployment POST times out after its body was sent. The selected official template uses random app names. Continue safely.
evals.expected_output: Treats the outcome as unknown, performs a read-only live footprint inventory, never generates a second random identity, and stops without retrying or using kubectl fallback

assertions.name: does-not-assume-failure
assertions.description: Recognizes that a timeout after submission may still have created resources

assertions.name: inventories-before-mutation
assertions.description: Checks recent Instances, Apps, and template resource kinds and names before any retry

assertions.name: prevents-duplicate-random-deploy
assertions.description: Preserves resolved identifiers and never renders a second random app name or host

assertions.name: stops-ambiguous-retry
assertions.description: If no footprint is visible, keeps the outcome unknown and does not retry or use kubectl fallback

evals.prompt: Deploy a Compose project with a README image acme/frontend:latest, a CI-published ghcr.io/acme/api:v2, and Compose services frontend, api, postgres:16, redis:stable, and a build-only worker. Every declared image selector resolves successfully.
evals.expected_output: Phase 2 inventories the whole project using README before CI before Compose, resolves each exact declared selector to a digest, preserves the database, cache, and build-only worker, and sends only the worker through Dockerfile/build preparation

assertions.name: uses-declared-evidence-order
assertions.description: Uses README, then CI publish evidence, then Compose; it does not guess an image from the GitHub owner/repository name

assertions.name: accepts-floating-selectors
assertions.description: Treats latest, stable, and v2 as valid source selectors and does not search for replacement version tags

assertions.name: does-not-prescreen-architecture
assertions.description: Reuses every exact selector that resolves to a digest without rejecting third-party images from architecture metadata

assertions.name: emits-immutable-references
assertions.description: Records reusable images as repository@sha256 digest references

assertions.name: preserves-complete-topology
assertions.description: Keeps frontend, api, PostgreSQL, Redis, and worker in the service inventory rather than filtering infrastructure

assertions.name: builds-per-service
assertions.description: Runs Phase 3 and Phase 4 only for the build-only worker instead of skipping or rebuilding the entire project

assertions.name: keeps-database-capability
assertions.description: Carries PostgreSQL into Phase 5 for an equivalent KubeBlocks conversion and preserves its application wiring

evals.prompt: Deploy a repository whose README declares app/image:2.1, but the manifest supports only linux/arm64. Its Dockerfile starts with FROM node:latest and the source is otherwise buildable for linux/amd64.
evals.expected_output: Phase 2 resolves and reuses the exact image selector without architecture pre-screening. If the live linux/amd64 runtime later reports an exact architecture mismatch, the available source is rebuilt for linux/amd64 and the replacement is pinned using the digest returned by Buildx

assertions.name: reuses-before-runtime-proof
assertions.description: Does not reject the digest-resolved image from manifest architecture metadata

assertions.name: does-not-rewrite-selector
assertions.description: Does not replace 2.1 with a guessed exact version

assertions.name: allows-floating-base
assertions.description: Does not classify FROM node:latest as a Dockerfile error by itself

assertions.name: builds-amd64-only-after-runtime-proof
assertions.description: Builds the available source with --platform linux/amd64 only after exact runtime evidence such as no matching manifest or exec format error

assertions.name: digests-built-image
assertions.description: Uses the immutable digest returned by Buildx for the replacement image
